title: Integrated Board, Power, Memory, Storage, Display, and Mobile Triage · Incident Repair Bay
summary: A realistic integrated board, power, memory, storage, display, and mobile triage ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in integrated hardware causal diagnosis and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Differentiate no power, no POST, no display, no boot device, boot failure, OS failure, sleep failure, and intermittent restart using safe observable evidence.
Correlate power source, rails, connectors, firmware, indicators, beep or diagnostic codes, CPU, cooling, board, peripherals, load, and thermal timing.
Correlate memory pressure and errors, storage detection, controller, cable, health, capacity, filesystem, encryption, backup, data custody, and changed load.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug No Boot Scope
steps.instruction:
debug case — A mobile workstation intermittently fails POST, throttles, loses storage, corrupts display, and recovers after cooling after several simultaneous repairs. The debug variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Differentiate no power, no POST, no display, no boot device, boot failure, OS failure, sleep failure, and intermittent restart using safe observable evidence.

Inspect the No Boot Scope trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug No Boot Scope.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for differentiate no power, no post, no display, no boot device, boot failure, os failure, sleep failure, and intermittent restart using safe observable evidence. For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug No Boot Scope, keep No Boot Scope connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For No Boot Scope, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Competency probe: Differentiate no power, no POST, no display, no boot device, boot failure, OS failure, sleep failure, and intermittent restart using safe observable evidence.

For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug No Boot Scope, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A blank display means the computer did not complete POST.

stimulus.title: No Boot Scope case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A mobile workstation intermittently fails POST, throttles, loses storage, corrupts display, and recovers after cooling after several simultaneous repairs. lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Differentiate no power, no POST, no display, no boot device, boot failure, OS failure, sleep failure, and intermittent restart using safe observable evidence. Artifact under review: integrated hardware causal diagnosis.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies No Boot Scope, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for No Boot Scope.

For inspect the failure record, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Integrated Hardware Proof
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Integrated Hardware Proof: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A mobile workstation intermittently fails POST, throttles, loses storage, corrupts display, and recovers after cooling after several simultaneous repairs. The debug variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Use one-change experiments, known-good controlled substitutions, fault injection or simulation, before-and-after measures, recurrence checks, rollback, and unresolved-risk escalation.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Integrated Hardware Proof.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Integrated Hardware Proof, keep Integrated Hardware Proof connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use one-change experiments, known-good controlled substitutions, fault injection or simulation, before-and-after measures, recurrence checks, rollback, and unresolved-risk escalation.
